kpis.label: Policy rate after the June 16, 2026 hike — highest since 1995
Since exit from easing (Mar 2024): Jul '24, Jan '25, Dec '25, Jun '26
Next MPM (2026) — decision Jul 31 with a new Outlook Report; first vote for Sato
Net board drift: dovish dissenters replaced; doves now accept gradual hikes
Asada (PM Takaichi's first pick) voted to hold in June 2026 — a new dovish pole

speakers.name: UEDA Kazuo
speakers.quote: "The Bank... will continue to raise the policy interest rate and adjust the degree of monetary accommodation." — Keidanren, Dec 25, 2025. Achievement of the 2% target "is steadily approaching."

speakers.name: TAMURA Naoki
speakers.shift: Consistently the board's most hawkish voice (≈ +2 throughout)
speakers.quote: "It is quite possible that, as early as this spring, the price stability target... can be judged to have been achieved"; hike impact so far "extremely limited"; neutral rate "at least around 1 percent." — Kanagawa, Feb 13, 2026.

speakers.name: TAKATA Hajime
speakers.shift: "Gear shift" advocate → proposed 1.0% hike at Jan 2026 MPM (≈ +1.5 → +2)
speakers.quote: "At the January 2026 MPM, I proposed that the Bank raise the policy interest rate to 1.0 percent as a further gear shift." — Kyoto, Feb 26, 2026.

speakers.name: HIMINO Ryozo
speakers.shift: Steady gradualist; intellectual groundwork for hikes (≈ +0.5 → +1)
speakers.quote: Mar 2026: policy "should gradually shift to a more neutral stance through moderate policy rate hikes."

speakers.name: UCHIDA Shinichi
speakers.quote: After the Aug 2024 rout, pledged no hikes amid market instability. Fronted the June 2026 presser during Ueda's hospitalization.

speakers.name: NOGUCHI Asahi
speakers.quote: "The most realistic approach... is to conduct policy interest rate hikes incrementally"; a pace that is "too slow... increases the risk of destabilizing economic activity and prices." — Oita, Nov 27, 2025.

speakers.name: NAKAGAWA Junko
speakers.shift: On-consensus throughout → reportedly called for a hike in Apr 2026 (≈ 0 → +0.5)
speakers.quote: "The Bank will continue to raise the policy interest rate... in response to improvement in economic and price developments." — Okayama, Nov 10, 2025. Succeeded by Sato Ayano.

speakers.name: KOEDA Junko
speakers.shift: Data-driven, leans hawkish from arrival (≈ +1); replaced dove Adachi
speakers.quote: "Underlying inflation is about 2 percent"; "the Bank needs to proceed with interest rate normalization... to avoid creating unintended distortions." — Niigata, Nov 20, 2025.

speakers.name: MASU Kazuyuki
speakers.shift: Supports normalization, stresses caution (≈ +0.5); replaced dove Nakamura
speakers.quote: "I am convinced that continuing with further policy interest rate hikes will be needed to complete the normalization"... but the Bank will "proceed cautiously." — Ehime, Feb 6, 2026.

speakers.name: ASADA Toichiro
speakers.role: Board Member (from Apr 2026; PM Takaichi's first pick)
speakers.shift: Sole dissenter at the June 2026 hike (≈ −1.5)
speakers.quote: Voted to hold at 0.75% at his second meeting, citing downside risks to production and employment.

board.record: Chairman; in the majority at all meetings. Absent (hospitalized) for the Jun 2026 vote.
Voted with the majority throughout; fronted Jun 2026 presser.
Dec 2024: proposed 0.5% · Jun 2025: opposed slowing the JGB taper · Sep & Oct 2025: proposed 0.75%.
Voted with the majority since joining.
Voted with the majority since joining.
Jun 2026: sole dissent against the hike to 1.00% (preferred hold), at his second meeting.
Joined end-June 2026 (replaced Nakagawa); no votes yet. Reported reflationist lean.
No formal dissents; reportedly urged a hike at the Apr 2026 hold.
Jul 2024: dissented against the hike to 0.25%.
Jul 2024 and Jan 2025: dissented against the hikes.
Dovish rhetoric but voted with the majority in window.

timeline.text: Rate raised to 0.25%; JGB purchase-reduction plan announced.
Global market rout; Deputy Governor Uchida calms markets — no hikes while markets are unstable.
Rate raised to 0.5% (highest since 2008), after Himino's and Ueda's signaling.
US tariff shock: Outlook Report cuts FY25 growth forecast from 1.1% to 0.5%; hiking cycle pauses.
JGB taper extended at gentler pace from Apr 2026; ETF/J-REIT sales decided (Sep). Jul: Japan–US trade agreement.
Holds, but Tamura and Takata dissent at both meetings, proposing 0.75%.
Takaichi government takes office; Oct Outlook: FY25 CPI 2.7%, FY26 1.8%, FY27 2.0%.
Rate raised to 0.75% (unanimous) — first hike in 11 months; 10-year JGB yield tops 2%.
Hold, but Takata proposes going to 1.0%. Jan Outlook restores pre-tariff forecasts.
Hold at 0.75% amid Iran-war worries; inflation forecast raised. Nakagawa reportedly calls for a hike.
Rate raised to 1.00% (7–1; Asada dissents), highest since Sep 1995, amid yen weakness and FX intervention.

mpm_calendar.notes: Next meeting. First vote for Sato Ayano; first meeting with the rate at 1.00%.
Released immediately after the MPM; full text 2pm next business day.
Full record of the 7–1 hike to 1.00%, incl. Asada's dissent.
No Outlook Report at this meeting.
Final meeting of 2026; no Outlook Report.

July 2026.context: Super-long supply amid post-hike curve repricing
Prices in the July MPM outcome
MPM day 1 — short end most exposed to the Jul 31 decision

August 2026.context: First 10y supply after the July MPM/Outlook
Breakeven read with CPI still near 2%+

fiscal.relevance: Revenue outturn frames the scale of any autumn supplementary budget.

fiscal.event: Ministries' FY2027 budget requests submitted to MOF
fiscal.relevance: First indication of spending pressure under the Takaichi administration.
A package on the scale of the 2025 'Comprehensive Economic Measures' would imply additional JGB issuance.

fiscal.event: Ruling-party tax reform outline for FY2027
fiscal.relevance: Sets the revenue side of the budget; signals the fiscal stance.

fiscal.event: FY2027 draft budget & FY2027 JGB Issuance Plan (cabinet approval)
fiscal.relevance: Determines gross issuance and maturity mix; coincides with BOJ deliberations on its post-Apr 2027 purchase plan — fiscal and monetary calendars intersect.
Budget deliberations begin; political friction can delay passage.
Required before the fiscal year starts April 1.

fiscal.event: Basic Policy on Economic and Fiscal Management ('honebuto')
fiscal.relevance: Sets the medium-term fiscal framework, incl. the primary-balance target.

fiscal.event: MOF meetings with JGB Market Special Participants / investors
fiscal.relevance: Typically precede issuance-mix changes; moved markets during the 2025 super-long selloff.
Sustained stimulus raises the salience of rating commentary for the long end.
